reasoning: By October 2025, LLM hallucinations remain a fundamentally unsolved issue, primarily because large language models are statistically based next-token predictors rather than ground-truth reasoners. The quotation from Emily Bender included in the question’s resolution criteria strongly signals that the bar for "solving" the problem is very high and grounded in a critical view of LLM capabilities. With only two months left before the end of 2025, the likelihood of an architectural breakthrough completely eliminating hallucinations in all use cases is functionally zero. The main remaining uncertainty is simply whether the market creator applies an unpredictably lenient subjective evaluation. Given the market estimate of 8% on October 16, 2025, I align slightly below that at 7%, accounting for the very short timeframe remaining to solve the problem securely.
